programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the death of a popular country singer murdered in his dressing room minutes before he was due to make an acceptance speech. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan discovers a manipulative killer posing as a psychiatrist, who uses hypnotism to lure his victims to their deaths

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An aggressive female pharmaceuticals representative stalks the hospital's new chief of staff, hoping to break up his marriage and have him all to herself - and it is not long before Dr Sloan has another murder investigation on his hands

programme.desc: Team Scorpion take on Mother Nature when a tsunami is set to strike a nuclear power plant in Mexico, while Toby secretly helps his ex-fiancee recover her stolen identity

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ziva's family farm in Israel is devastated by an explosion, and the former NCIS agent is reported dead. Determined to seek justice, Tony ignores orders to stay off the case and sets out to track down Trent Kort, the British national and ex-CIA agent who remains the prime suspect

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A traumatised marine escapes from a psychiatric unit, convinced he is still fighting in Iraq. The agents believe his erratic behaviour indicates post-traumatic stress disorder and try to calmly talk him round before restraining him. However, blood tests reveal high levels of steroids in his system - meaning someone is using him as a human lab rat. Starring Lauren Holly, Sean Murray and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Muslim US Marine is found seemingly shot dead near a mosque that has been linked to an al-Qaeda recruiter. Ducky refuses to perform an autopsy out of respect for the man's religion, but Abby runs tests that reveal his demise was actually due to poisoning from a rare octopus venom. Meanwhile, McGee goes into the mosque intending to plant a concealed microphone - but discovers someone has had the same idea

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team set up a stake-out at a storage facility to catch a thief suspected of selling hi-tech Navy equipment on the black market, but end up witnessing a murder. To make matters worse, $90million worth of radar gear goes missing while they are distracted. Meanwhile, Ducky asks Abby to run a series of mysterious blood tests, raising suspicions that he is harbouring a secret

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A suspect in a drug-smuggling case is seemingly mauled to death by his own dog, which is immediately scheduled to be destroyed - but Abby refuses to hand it over to Navy officials and launches her own investigation. Meanwhile, Gibbs continues to worry about Shepard's health, convinced she and Ducky are hiding something. Drama, starring Pauley Perrette, Mark Harmon and Lauren Holly

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion take on Mother Nature when a tsunami is set to strike a nuclear power plant in Mexico, while Toby secretly helps his ex-fiancee recover her stolen identity

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen has been placed on suspension after going rogue and shooting dead the Chameleon, leaving the once tight-knit team shaken and divided. Meanwhile, former operations manager Hetty, who quit over her colleague's actions, is adjusting to life in retirement. Crime drama set in the high-stakes world of the Office of Special Projects, starring Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J and Linda Hunt

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a former marine killed during a drone strike on a terrorist target - but with the victim's family refusing to believe he would have returned to the Middle East willingly, NCIS officers try to determine why he was associating with a notorious Taliban bomb-maker. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Four seemingly unrelated individuals are killed in a controlled bombing at a Pasadena restaurant, but as the NCIS officers dig deeper, they discover a link between the victims - all were members of an experimental strategy game developed by the Office of Naval Intelligence. The question is, who wanted them dead - and why? Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A hit-and-run driver kills a man working on the campaign for an aspiring senator, but it soon becomes a case of national security when closer examination of the video footage makes it clear he was murdered. The agents' job is to figure out whether it is an isolated incident or part of a politically motivated plot that might put others in danger - and most of the pressure is on Kensi, who takes the victim's old job and goes undercover in the campaign office

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. Hanna receives a call for help from a former CIA colleague, only to find the man dead in an apparent suicide. Further investigations reveal his phone call was pre-recorded, putting the team onto the trail of a conspiracy that sees Deeks and Kensi go undercover at a dog show - complete with prize poodle - to look into a breeder the victim was investigating before he died. LL Cool J, Chris O'Donnell, Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Ben and Callum try to cover for Sally after she is involved in a drink-driving collision that endangers the life of a teenager. As suspicions mount back at the station, Callum visits a witness and puts pressure on him to keep quiet about seeing the guilty PC getting into her car after an evening's drinking. Victoria Alcock guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Rachel and Roger save the life of a drug dealer found bound and beaten after reports of a disturbance. The trail leads to Jay Newman, whose family are in the care of DS Elizabeth Marsh (guest star Jackie Clune) of the witness protection programme. Despite appeals to leave Jay out of the investigation because of the threat of reprisals, Grace presses him for information, putting his family at risk

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Yvonne sets off for Barton Street Station to face formal manslaughter charges but has a run-in with a drunk en route and ends up hitting him - which won't exactly count in her favour when she appears in court. Jo Masters discovers one of Phil's informants is dealing in drugs with the collusion of a crooked customs officer, while Roger and Dan find a missing schoolgirl in woodlands and realise she has just given birth - but can't see any sign of her baby

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jacob is shocked to learn his lawyer wife Naomi (guest star Kim Thomson) has been mugged in a dark car park. It emerges this was not a random attack but the work of someone with a personal grudge seeking revenge. The search begins for Bobby Henshaw, whose younger brother was knocked down and killed by Alistair Finch, who walked away scot-free from court after Naomi successfully defended him

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Boxing Day sees heavy snowfall covering the country and the adverse weather causes major disruption across Poplar as roads become blocked and pipes freeze meaning families have to queue outside for water from standpipes. Sister Julienne must inform a family about the death of their loved one and uncovers an uncomfortable truth, while Valerie helps an unmarried couple living in a caravan prepare for their baby, but unforeseen complications knock her confidence in her ability as a midwife. Guest starring Anita Dobson

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives of Nonnatus House return, with new nurse Barbara, who is anxious to prove herself - only to get off on the wrong foot after a late-night drinks party. A family of severely neglected children is discovered, so Trixie strives to restore their health and bring happiness into their lives, leaving Tom filled with admiration for the woman he loves, while Sister Evangelina finally undergoes tests for her abdominal pains. Shelagh finds she is at the surgery almost as much as she is at home, so the Turners come up with a solution which, for 1960, proves somewhat ground-breaking

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara faces her most challenging case so far when she treats a first-time mother, only for the birth not to go according to plan. A new nurse arrives and soon makes an impression on Sister Evangelina, while Sister Julienne is worried about the increasing financial pressure on Nonnatus House, but doesn't bank on a mystery benefactor coming to the rescue. Jenny Agutter and Pam Ferris star, with Linda Bassett (East Is East) joining the cast

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Tilly Trotter
programme.desc: Part one of two. Period drama about a 19-year-old girl who is attacked after a party celebrating the engagement of a farmer for whom she has feelings. Starring Carli Norris

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Tilly Trotter
programme.desc: Concluding part. Following the destruction of their home by the McGraths, Tilly and her grandmother are forced to live a life of solitude. Tilly and Katie Drew begin working at Sopwith's mine. Despite the hardships of life in the pit, she enjoys feeling like part of the family. Period drama, starring Carli Norris and Sarah Jane Foster

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne oversees the opening of a family planning clinic at the local community centre, leading mother-of-three Wilma Goddens to start taking the contraceptive pill behind her husband's back. Barbara is distraught when she discovers that her father has accepted a missionary posting in New Guinea for the next three years so will not be able to officiate at her wedding, while Violet suffers terribly with hot flushes, Christopher asks Trixie to meet his daughter, and Delia's heartache may finally be over

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A newlywed, recently arrived in England from New Zealand, sees disturbing visions of a strangled corpse in the hallway of her dream home. Joan Hickson stars as the amateur detective determined to get to the bottom of the mystery

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy
programme.desc: As the parish council election approaches, the townsfolk are on tenterhooks, awaiting the next post from 'the Ghost of Barry Fairbrother'. Who is behind the internet messages and what will they say next? In an attempt to smooth things over, Samantha throws a dinner party for all the warring factions, but when Howard and Shirley unexpectedly arrive things quickly spiral out of control. Across in the Fields, Terri seems to be over her addiction, giving Krystal hope of a new future for her family. Drama adapted from the novel by JK Rowling, starring Michael Gambon, Keeley Hawes, Abigail Lawrie and Keeley Forsyth

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: A pregnant patient triggers Tara's anxiety and PTSD, while Meryl and Jarrod seek Rod Eagles help, but his price may just be too high for Meryl

programme.desc: A property war breaks out in Chigwell as man-eating neighbour Dorien and Tracey's son Garth compete for the flat above Sharon's cafe. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Matthew Savage

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Holby's MP falls downstairs during a scuffle with a constituent and is rushed to the ED, where his condition deteriorates. Nathan hires an undercover journalist to bring about the hospital's downfall, only for his plans to be foiled by Charlie. Josh's short temper continues to cause problems

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Suspected rapist Tom Norris stands trial but walks away from court a free man after the victim's plucky effort to give evidence crumbles under tough interrogation from Banksy's wife Naomi Woods. Sam is obsessed with nailing her suspect and picks over the case in minute detail, but forensic evidence offers no help. When a similar crime is committed at the same scene, it is Banksy who realises they must have been looking in the wrong place

programme.desc: Tiffany still has no hot water at the flat and tries to find somewhere to bath Courtney; Peggy at the Vic helps out. Bianca is refused her stall by the temporary market inspector

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Calum becomes more enthusiastic when he meets Emma Styles, the new veterinary student. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy, Christopher Timothy and John McGlynn

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealer agrees to buy a 19th-century dresser for £65, and finds it is worth £20,000 - but unfortunately, he is not the only one who knows its true value and Beth is conned into parting with it. Ian McShane and Diane Parish star

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester and William rely on their son-in-law's knowledge of French law when they both find themselves in trouble with the authorities. Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers star

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom's new fixation over personal hygiene and laundry leads Diana to suspect he has a new girlfriend. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.desc: Hyacinth is thrown into dismay when her beloved father goes to the register office to be married, and things go from bad to worse when her neighbour Emmett comes face to face with her uncouth relatives, Daisy, Onslow and Rose

programme.desc: Truly investigates the case of a bus that disappeared between stops. Whimsical comedy, starring Frank Thornton, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: A man is found dead in a container that has washed up on the beach and Davey is convinced it was an unfortunate accident, but Jane fears darker forces may be responsible

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: Framed for murder and betrayed by one of his closest colleagues, the detective reluctantly accepts the aid of the calculating Alice Morgan, and turns to his love rival Mark North to help clear his name. Meanwhile, DSU Rose Teller struggles to come to terms with Luther's apparent crimes, and is forced to take ruthless action to bring him to justice. Thriller, starring Idris Elba, Ruth Wilson and Saskia Reeves

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra takes drastic measures to prevent Jack exacting revenge on the hit-and-run driver who killed his wife. With the team's future under threat, she tries desperately to keep the truth about the incident from shady new recruit DS Karen Hardwick, while working on a 10-year-old murder case to restore the squad's reputation. Amanda Redman and Lynda Bellingham star

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: An audacious killer taunts the members of Glasgow CID by bumping off the owner of the hotel where their annual dinner is being held - while proceedings are in full swing and the place is full of off-duty detectives. Drama, starring James MacPherson and Blythe Duff

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The success of a new business venture causes Sharon and Tracey's lives to take a new direction. Meanwhile, Dorien advises a depressed Garth, who's desperate to find out what life's all about. With Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Lulu is frustrated when Sacha prevents her from assisting Michael in surgery, and she deliberately places a patient in jeopardy in an effort to undermine him. Meanwhile, Chantelle unwittingly puts Elizabeth in grave danger while trying to repair her relationship with Simone, and Sahira worries about Greg as he tries to forget his past by flirting endlessly with the hospital's female staff

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part Christmas special from 2006, tensions run high as staff deal with an influx of emergencies on one of the busiest days of the year, and Ellen's nightmare continues as she undergoes an MRI scan to find out whether her cancer has returned. Laura brings in her daughter Rosie, complaining of stomach pains, but when the pair's story fails to add up it becomes apparent all is not what it seems. A dejected Josh comes under fire from Dixie as he puts his life at risk to save a woman trapped in a frozen pond. However, as events spiral out of control back at the hospital, he finds himself staring death in the face for a second time

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sam struggles to get a lead on Tom Norris in the rape case and new information from Naomi throws the spotlight on his housemate Dan Cox, who once covered for his friend to help him escape sexual harassment charges. The theory that the pair are working together to prey on young women seems more plausible - until Norris turns up bound and beaten in the boot of Cox's car

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Lenny is woken by an angry George. Lorraine arrives back in the Square with Joe and goes to the Vic to see Grant. Martin is beaten up by Shane's gang

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin's involvement with the gang escalates as they plan a heist of the community centre

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: The medic temporarily hangs up his stethoscope to intervene in a confrontation between Irish farm labourers and irate locals who blame them for a typhus outbreak, resolving matters with a combination of tough talking and common sense. David Rintoul stars, with Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A retired sheepdog takes to rounding up cars, and James is challenged to a race. Rural drama based on the novels of James Herriot, starring Christopher Timothy, Peter Davison and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Lady Jane makes a spectacular reappearance in Lovejoy's life just as he decides it is time to settle down - leaving him forced to choose between her and Charlotte. Meanwhile, faithful companion Tinker prepares to retire just as Eric Catchpole makes an unscheduled return to the fold. Last ever episode of the comedy drama, starring Ian McShane, Dudley Sutton, Caroline Langrishe, Phyllis Logan and Chris Jury

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester lends a hand with a coach tour for British buyers of French homes, while William has plumbing worries. The pair are settling in well, but the good life turns sour when their daughter visits. Comedy, starring Julia Mackenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tests confirm Tom has prostate trouble and Harvey is excited when a representative from Bayview has been chosen to attend the Council of European Old People. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth dreads the idea of attending Onslow's birthday celebrations. However, she changes her mind when she learns a limousine will be arriving to take her to the party. Starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler goes on the run when he has a tipple too much of Auntie's home-made wine, leaving Compo, Truly and Barry to track him down. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detectives' case against a violent criminal is thrown into jeopardy by the murder of a key witness. Determined to secure a conviction, they immerse themselves in the seedy underworld of a seaside town, and as they press for answers, Havers is brought face to face with her childhood memories. Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra's private and professional worlds become intertwined when her mother has a fall that means she needs to go into a care home. Her first choice is revealed to be the scene of a suspicious death a year earlier, leading Jack, Gerry and Brian to go undercover at the home. Drama, guest starring June Whitfield, Sheila Hancock and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The team is assigned to protect murderer Susan Kellar, whose recent release from jail has provoked a storm of protest and forced her to go into hiding for her own safety. Unfortunately, they find themselves unable to protect her family from the lethal attentions of a vigilante. Feature-length crime drama, starring James Macpherson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Pentecost
programme.desc: Live service from All Saints Church, Woodford Wells, led by Rev Paul Harcourt, Becky Harcourt and Yasmin Omotosho, with music from All Saints' worship group, led by Martin Donovan

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley discovers unsung heroes, including a taxi driver helping his community through the pandemic, and a fire chief encouraging others to follow in her footsteps

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Santiago
programme.desc: On the final stretch of the Camino de Santiago pilgrimage, Ed Byrne, JJ Chalmers and the Rev Kate Bottley take a dip in the freezing water within a beautiful wooded valley. Neil Morrissey and Heather Small's snoring means some choose to sleep outside, Debbie McGee battles blisters, while journalist Raphael Rowe discovers it is the company of others that is spurring him on. As they near the end of their journey, it remains to be seen what the experience of re-enacting the 780km pilgrimage has meant to the participants and whether they can identify any lasting change within themselves. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Pam Rhodes present an episode for Pentecost Sunday, meeting an ultra-marathon runner and learning about monastic life and a biblical-inspired cartoon

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a London couple who have a £700,000 budget to leave the capital and put down retirement roots in East Sussex. While in the county, Nicki rides the Hastings East Hill Cliff Railway, the UK's steepest funicular railway

programme.title: Frozen (2013)
programme.desc: A princess with the power to freeze anything she touches loses control of her abilities and plunges the kingdom into a perpetual winter. She goes into hiding, but her sister remains determined that she can be saved from her own magic and sets off on a quest to find her, aided by a mountain man and a living snowman. Disney animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Kristen Bell and Idina Menzel

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of painted wolves on the banks of the Zambezi river, as an ageing matriarch faces a challenge for supremacy from her own daughter, who has grown to lead a pack of her own, which now needs to expand into new territory. With human-occupied land penning them in, the two packs are forced to fight for the little resources available

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Special episode devoted to community gardens and planting wildflowers, exploring their benefits both to the well-being of the people who make use of them and to the environment, with positive influences on climate change and bee populations. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor help Bristol locals to create a community plot, and Charlotte Smith visits a blooming project in Glasgow. Sean Fletcher reports on a field to fork project involving children in Northern Ireland, and Helen Skelton meets a botanist working to preserve endangered wildflowers

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items include Chippendale tables with a hefty price tag, an original Punch and Judy set used to entertain children on the lawns of the hall, and an intriguing necklace linked to a secret society. There is also rare rock memorabilia, including a poster of Mick Jagger by Andy Warhol

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie stirs up controversy in the community when she speaks out on behalf of a patient who is pregnant with her fifth child, but whose personal life is far from stable. Dr Turner and Sister Frances try to help a woman who is refusing medical treatment through the last weeks of her pregnancy, and Nancy reveals a secret about her background that could threaten her relationship with Nonnatus House

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Fanny is furious at the decadent lifestyle Linda is leading in Paris with her lover, while also resenting her own unhappy marriage. However, as Europe stands on the brink of war, the divided cousins must learn to put their differences aside before it is too late

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the final matches of the Premier League season, including Leicester City v Tottenham Hotspur at King Power Stadium and Liverpool v Crystal Palace at Anfield. Leicester prevailed 2-0 away to Spurs in December and will have been looking for another good result to end a strong campaign on a high. The Reds thrashed Palace 7-0 in the reverse fixture to spend Christmas at the top of the table, but their form dipped dramatically following that match. Plus, Arsenal v Brighton & Hove Albion, Aston Villa v Chelsea, Fulham v Newcastle United, Leeds United v West Bromwich Albion, Manchester City v Everton, Sheffield United v Burnley, West Ham United v Southampton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester United

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down 10 of the most memorable escapes from Premier League relegation

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three terraced properties feature in this programme, with Martin Roberts in Birmingham, Dion Dublin in Bradford and Martel Maxwell in Dover

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on a nurse ripping off the NHS for £1000s, and the boss of a recycling firm who helped himself to millions in government money

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A climber plunges hundreds of feet down a mountain in the Lake District, coming to rest above a precipice. Plus, a driver ploughs into a car blocking the fast lane of a motorway

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross visits the Ardingly International Antiques Fair, with experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine. He also visits a business that conserves historical artifacts

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma tries to help a patient who could be hiding something over the course of several consultations. Lucy Benjamin (Detectorists) guest stars in a special two-hander episode

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants David, Raj, Jo and Sophy vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a retired army couple to find a secluded new home in Norfolk, and also learns about the trade in the world's most expensive spice, saffron

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers come up with competing designs for a Bristol couple on a budget of £6500, The couple want to transform their large, disordered and overgrown garden into an urban suntrap reminiscent of their holidays in the Mediterranean

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques dealer Paul Martin makes his Road Trip debut alongside seasoned tripper Raj Bisram. A mysterious giant and a very cute dog also feature in this South Downs adventure

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come up with new designs for a Hampshire garden, and must please both owners by incorporating a Greek design and an outdoor model railway

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ruby is unimpressed with Martin's hangover and ends up having to go to a doctor's appointment by herself. At the gym launch, Sharon spots Zack turning on the charm with an important potential client, and Kheerat promises to have a word. Kat returns home from the hospital with Phil by her side, and Jean warns her about the Mitchells

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: The hotel is plunged into chaos by the death of a guest, leading to all manner of mayhem as Basil tries to conceal the corpse from the other occupants. Classic comedy, guest starring Geoffrey Palmer, with John Cleese and Prunella Scales

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: It is the day of Jack's memorial, and while the police investigation ramps up, the pact begins to fracture. Max realises what Arwel has done and arrests him, prompting a disbelieving Louie to confront him over dark family secrets. Anna's relationship with Max is strained when he realises their savings account has been emptied, and she is forced to weave a web of lies to keep her secret. As another blackmail message comes through, she reaches breaking point and decides to uncover who is behind the threats

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: Reeling from their fight, both Tom and Jessie struggle at work. Jessie reconsiders if London is the place for her. Sitcom, starring Rose Matafeo and Nikesh Patel

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Alice never told her childhood sweetheart Leo that she swapped their relationship for a life of polyamory. She's now ready to reveal the truth - how will he react and could there be an exciting new future for them? Nas is desperate to win back his ex Vent, but will his jealousy get the better of him once again? Fatima wants to explain why she ghosted her ex Alan and see if there's a possibility of a second chance. Can he forgive her and agree to a fresh start?

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: RuPaul and fellow judges Michelle Visage and Rhys Nicholson set Australia and New Zealand's aspiring superstars a special sewing challenge, testing their ability to create spectacular costumes from scratch

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Alexandra Burke, as they assist two brothers from Middlesbrough in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a scientist, a prison officer and an electrician

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is surprised to discover the high expense involved in buying a patch of grass in London, before heading off to Greater Manchester to look at a tenanted, terraced property that leaves a lot to be desired. Meanwhile, Martel Maxwell visits Cumbria to look round a two-bed semi with an interesting roof

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The investigators look into an IT support worker shamelessly helping himself to hospital computers and a gang illegally fly-tipping on an industrial scale

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Emergency services rush to find a pregnant woman who has collapsed on a remote moor, and dash-cam footage captures the moment a driver falls asleep at the wheel

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts from Lincolnshire Antiques and Home Show as experts Jonathan Pratt and Gary Pe assist the teams racing around the fair pursuing potential purchases for profit at auction. Eric also shares some top tips on collecting Clarice Cliff pottery

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Luca is on the hunt for Jonno, but he may not like what he finds. Al tries to mend the difficult relationship between a wildly different mother and son

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Dion, James, Marilyn and Penny vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah visits Scotland, making her way to Dumfries & Galloway with a married couple from London who are seeking a life-changing move and have a £350,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet Liz, Geoff and their daughter Lois in Yate, Gloucestershire, where the hope is they can use a £4,500 budget to create a garden with a nautical theme in memory of Liz's father, who was known locally as ''The Pirate'

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The trip continues in Wiltshire for debutant Paul Martin and seasoned chum Raj Bisram, but a risky buy of antique jelly moulds causes an auction wobble for one expert

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. With Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With her shocked guests watching on, Kheerat tries to apologise but Sharon directs her fury toward her brother - she wants Zack to pack his bags and leave. A comment from Vi has Kim worrying that she's getting old before her time, so she promptly asks out a man out for dinner, and Jean implores Ruby to show Lily some love

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The day arrives for Dom to overcome the traumas of the recent past by stepping up to consultant level. But how far is he willing to go to ensure his path to power? A desperate Kian may have to betray Fletch's trust to get what he wants, while Louis bears the brunt of Max's fear over her diagnosis

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man in Scotland whose online girlfriend in Ghana has demanded thousands of pounds, but has never visited him

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: Nancy learns some disturbing news about Richard and confronts him, only to be rocked by his admission. Unsure how to remedy the situation, she's drawn closer to Fr Martin, and makes a rash decision. Cat and Tish try to get rid of Louie's stolen goods, while Louie struggles with the news about Arwel and faces a terrible dilemma. As the police make a significant breakthrough, Max tries to grill Anna on what she knows about Jack's death - putting their marriage on the line

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The MUAs enter the world of special effects, as they are tasked with recreating looks from the smash hit crime drama Peaky Blinders - working at a prestigious make-up academy with guest judge, Peaky's Bafta-winning head make-up designer Loz Scathio. This week's Creative Brief is based on the seven deadly sins, and while one artist's gruesome looks bring out sloth, envy and wrath in very imaginative ways, one MUA's literal interpretation of greed falls flat

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: When 22-year-old Bobbie Edwards had to close her gym Colwyn Bay under lockdown, she launched women-only glutes boot camp courses instead and here reveals the secrets of its success. Bridgend-based entrepreneur Scott Magill reveals how he struck lockdown-gold with his doorstep doughnut delivery service

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma has a big mess to take care of on her arrival home, Norman agrees to seek help, and Dylan receives shocking news about Emma's health

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Caleb and Dylan are confronted by a dangerous proposition, and Sheriff Romero decides to confront Norma

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to the historic market town of Oakham in Rutland, where the Bloomers have to cope with major roadworks if they are to complete their project

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don adds flowers to act as pollinators and creates an alpine trough. Adam Frost helps comedian Susan Calman with her garden, and Nick Bailey reveals a simple way to attract wildlife

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith head to the Peak District to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the UK's first National Park. Matt joins a pioneering scheme to restore ruined farm buildings and meets 'legend' of the Peak District Gordon Miller, one of the area's early rangers and a last link with those who brought about the National Park. Charlotte channels her inner artist to help to create a giant anniversary art mural and records the special sounds of the Park's waterways, while Joe Crowley investigates new pollution laws causing controversy in the countryside

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond is on the hunt for signs of spring with nature writer Jim Crumley. Arlene Stuart helps out some Edinburgh toads that are on the move for love, while crofter and musician Colin Macleod is busy lambing. Plus it's the season to cook al fresco again and Dougie and Nick Nairn have the perfect recipe for a barbecue - teriyaki kebabs

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty joins Carole and Mairi at Beechgrove as they explore sustainable hanging basket options, including an upcycled bird cage. Brian reveals the next stage of his no dig converted lawn at Old Scone

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces recipes that can be prepared quickly, including Nigel Slater's herb butter squid, James Martin's icebox cake and Mary Berry's chocolate tray bake

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Comedian Ed Gamble joins food critic Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and cook and broadcaster Rachel Khoo to select the menu's final flourish - the dessert course. This is the last chance the chefs have of craft a course at the banquet celebrating British innovation, so tensions are running high as the rivals cook against each other for the final time. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andi Oliver presents the series finale - an outdoor banquet at Jodrell Bank in Cheshire. The star-studded guests, including Professor Sarah Gilbert from Oxford's Covid vaccination programme. must vote for the overall winning chef

programme.title: Live Swimming
programme.desc: Live coverage of the opening session of the final day at Danube Arena in Budapest, as the first major swimming event to take place since 2019 continues

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the final round of Premier League fixtures

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage of the opening Diamond League meeting of the season, held at Gateshead International Stadium. British 1500m record-holder Laura Muir is among those scheduled to compete, while an elite field in the women's 100m is set to feature the likes of Dina Asher-Smith, Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce and Elaine Thompson-Herah, and Mondo Duplantis, Sam Kendricks and Piotr Lisek should be involved in a competitive men's pole vault

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: Desperate for success and tempted by the lucrative rewards brought by towing the line, Jimmy White joined Barry Hearn in the hope he could be turned into a champion like his new stablemate Steve Davis. With White as the hot-favourite and with the crowd urging him on against 21-year-old Stephen Hendry, the final of 1990 world championship turned out to be a pivotal moment in British snooker and paved the way for what was to come in the next decade

programme.title: The Crucible: 40 Golden Snooker Years
programme.desc: Steve Davis goes back to the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield to celebrate 40 years of the world snooker championship, talking to some of the players forever ingrained in the sport's history. The venue has become the home of snooker since the championships moved there in 1977, helping to create memorable moments and scenes of drama that live on to this day. Featuring interviews with former champions Stephen Hendry and Dennis Taylor, six-time runner-up and fans' favourite Jimmy White, and celebrity snooker addicts Richard Osman and Stephen Fry

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: While Skully and Khadijah deal with a devastating blow, Leon and Fatback have to lie low. Teddy tells Franklin to face up to some incredibly tough decisions that test his loyalty

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: While Franklin is dealing with the fallout of Leon's actions, Teddy receives news that tears his world apart. No-one is ready when Khadijah takes matters into her own hands

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A major incident strikes Holby and with pupils from Hanssen's old school fighting for life, Josh worries that the CEO is struggling to cope. Will he be brave enough to ask for help before it's too late? Secret addict Kian's focus is frayed when asked to choose between a patient and surrogate son Andrei, and inexperienced doctor Jeong is thrown in at the deep end when Dom asks him to diagnose a mystery condition

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott head to the south coast of England for the final leg of their challenge, where Irina finds out about the history of beach huts while in Bournemouth, Dorset. Irita also fancies a table she knows Tim will hate, while Tim purchases a fine Chinese vase. However, the clock is ticking - the world's oldest clock at Salisbury Cathedral - which chimes for Tim

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques and Collectable fair. Presenter Charlie Ross gets shivers down his spine when he gets to find out more about some spooky antiques. Later, he and the contenders head to the auction in Derby

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts a special episode reflecting on the aftermath of the Second World War filmed at Coventry Cathedral, in which people bring in items that date back to the end of the conflict and reveal the personal stories behind them. Featuring a ballgown and a set of handmade playing cards that offer a glimpse into the final days of the war in the Far East, a fragile teacup that survived the bombing of Dresden, and a scrapbook documenting life in post-war Berlin

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts a game show in which pairs of contestants go head to head attempting to crack a series of passwords, in a bid to win £2,500

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Twenty-four chefs compete for a chance to show off their culinary skills at a banquet honouring 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. This week it's the turn of three chefs from London and the South East who are all first timers in the competition. Tom Kemble, Mike Reid and Selin Kiazim meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their summery starters. A dish celebrating childhood summer memories takes on a complicated courgette dish and a technically tricky take on the cucumber sandwich

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mike Reid shocks with when he pairs his fish with white chocolate and strawberry. Selin Kiazim's dish uses barbecued octopus and potato rostis as the central elements, while Tom Kemble creates poached buttered trout, but will it be enough to impress the show's veteran chef?

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: As the end of the decade approaches, Sarah and George prepare to leave for his embassy posting in Israel, and Sarah tries one more time to reconcile Jack and Carolyn. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Exploring the London's surprising and secret wildlife, including seals, parakeets, badgers, foxes and even pelicans who have made London their home. The programme also focuses on the admirers of these animals, from Billingsgate fish porters and Indian chefs to Crayfish Bob, who scours the canals for foreign species

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: Zoologist Lucy Cooke and wildlife cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson explore the nation's wildernesses to reveal the wildlife that exists there, beginning with its rivers

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Elizabeth Talbot and Philip Serrell are at Tilbury Docks in Essex in search of items to take off to auction, while presenter Paul Martin uncovers the history of Britain's biggest gunpowder factory.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels in Greater London, beginning on its south-eastern fringe in the village of Chislehurst and ending up in Kennington to learn about the childhood of Charlie Chaplin

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares pork loin stuffed with oregano and Parma ham, served with mock mash and vignarola. She extols the virtues of spelt spaghetti, which she accompanies with an olive and anchovy sauce that can be made without cooking, and also demonstrates her recipe for sambuca kisses, a doughnut-like sweet

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by David Baddiel, Adjoa Andoh, Phil Davis and Katherine Ryan, who share favourite books as well as reviewing the week's two club picks - The Girl with the Louding Voice by Abi Dare, and Sixteen Horses by Greg Buchanan

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon returns with the property makeover show in which cutting-edge virtual reality and visual effects enable people to see what the future of their home could look like, before it is built in reality. In the first edition architects Laura Jane Clark and Julian McIntosh compete to remodel Shelley and Steven's three-bedroom semi-detached home in Bromley, south-east London

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: It is Mother's Day and Julia is feeling completely ignored, while Meg's family have gone way over the top in reaction to her cancer diagnosis. Anne is beside herself with excitement at the prospect of a day out with her Mammy, while Liz and Kevin try to avoid mum stuff altogether with a trip to the countryside. Amanda's mother Felicity travels from Cheltenham for a gloriously passive-aggressive celebratory lunch. Guest starring Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Felix is pretty certain his wife is up to something with her boss Dmitri, so he decides to hire some help to prove things one way or the other. When professional lip-reader Iris arrives with her powerful binoculars, the pair stake out a hotel bedroom and start to gather their evidence. Darkly comic tale, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Sian Clifford

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Pointless co-host Richard Osman and former Conservative Party chair Sayeeda Warsi joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: As Cesare bids the club a fond farewell, Mark surprises the squad by announcing coach Chris Booth as caretaker manager - infuriating Petey, who cannot believe he has been passed over for the job. Mattie is finally selected to start a match, Benji employs a social media manager to increase his online followers and Chris Booth takes on a sports psychologist in an attempt to boost team performance

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: Chris Booth's methods are bringing results but that doesn't stop Petey plotting to bring an end to his run of success. Jack gets a girlfriend and plans a special party with a romantic surprise, Mattie tries to protect Chris from a shocking revelation and Benji has had enough of his social media manager and decides to fire him. Football comedy, starring Jake Short, Jack McMullen, Shaquille Ali-Yebuah and Theo Barklem-Biggs

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith head to the Peak District to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the UK's first National Park. Matt joins a pioneering scheme to restore ruined farm buildings and meets 'legend' of the Peak District Gordon Miller, one of the area's early rangers and a last link with those who brought about the National Park. Charlotte channels her inner artist to help to create a giant anniversary art mural and records the special sounds of the Park's waterways, while Joe Crowley investigates new pollution laws causing controversy in the countryside

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: A journey through the musician's life in 10 defining photos, with their secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew the musician best. The pictures show Lennon's lifelong search to find his place in the world, documenting his disaffection with living the life of a Beatle and his relationship with the woman who became the love of his life, the avant-garde artist Yoko Ono

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented times, capturing it in the year spent reacting to the pandemic. With nearly five million people now waiting for treatment in England alone, doctors are forced to take incredibly difficult decisions as they battle to save lives and fight for patients to have the treatment they desperately need

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross visits the Ardingly International Antiques Fair, with experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine. He also visits a business that conserves historical artifacts

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers come up with competing designs for a Bristol couple on a budget of £6500, The couple want to transform their large, disordered and overgrown garden into an urban suntrap reminiscent of their holidays in the Mediterranean

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don plants out his tomatoes and shows viewers what they can feed their plants. Arit Anderson looks into the use of peat in the horticultural industry, and Sue Kent reveals the progress on her allotment

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents highlights and analysis from the latest fixtures, including Warrington Wolves v Castleford Tigers at the Halliwell Jones Stadium

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Mike Reid, Selin Kiazim and Tom Kemble continue the battle for a place at the Taste of Summer banquet, celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. This time, the three chefs are tasked to create a main course, with each choosing to use aspects of traditional summer barbecues as their inspiration

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Selin Kiazim, Mike Reid and Tom Kemble continue the battle to cook at a Taste of Summer banquet celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. As each of them prepare their desserts, they will all be out to impress as only two can make it through to tomorrow's London and South East final

programme.title: Miniature Britain
programme.desc: Biologist George McGavin travels around Britain with a revolutionary new microscope camera to provide a close-up view of the natural world. The film reveals how caterpillars' feet have hooks that anchor them upside down to leaves, butterfly and moth wings are a kaleidoscope of colourful scales that deter predators, and birds' feathers have thousands of hooks that zip together to keep them airborne. The film-makers also show miniature life in cities, such as creatures grazing the moss on pavements and the legions of dust mites scavenging for food in everyday homes

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: Zoologist Lucy Cooke and wildlife cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson explore the nation's forests, and see how the pine marten in Scotland is helping animals in Wales

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott assess items brought into Newby Hall in North Yorkshire. Mark comes across an interesting Clarice Cliff cake plate and Michael enjoys an ancient Chinese urn. Presenter Paul Martin takes in the splendours of the country house's Adams interiors

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through the capital in the heart of London's East End. First, Michael explores Hackney Wick, where he hears about The Daily Worker newspaper's origins, before heading to London's West End to hunt down London's oldest surviving Indian restaurant, Veeraswamy. Here, he tries his hand at making chapatis, under the watchful eye of the restaurant's master chef

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts and Dr Yasmin Khan investigate the excavation of a vast Georgian cemetery near Euston Station to make way for the HS2 rail link. They discover what the site reveals about the lives of the people who were buried there, from the founders of Christie's auction house to a small child who was accidentally buried alive

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan are broadcasting live from Wild Ken Hill in Norfolk, as cameras capture the dramas of such birds as avocets and barn owls. Elsewhere, Iolo Williams encounters some red deer at Alladale Wilderness Reserve in Scotland, and Gillian Burke dips into the vast waters of Strangford Lough in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Cameras follow medical staff working in the maternity unit at the University Hospital Coventry. In addition to delivering over 5,500 babies every year, the specialised unit also pioneer tests and treatments as they undertake vital research into the causes of miscarriages and stillbirths

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: David Mitchell, Noel Fielding and Holly Walsh join Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme of 'night' in an extended edition of the panel show. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Identical twin brothers George and Peter who fled Nazi Germany in 1939 search for the family of the man who helped them escape. Plus, Clare, who housed 21 child refugees from wartorn Bosnia, looks for their mystery schoolgirl translator Petra, whose language skills allowed the children to properly communicate and share the horrors they had fled. Presented by Anita Rani

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: More than 25 years ago, Panorama reporter Martin Bashir secured his global scoop interview with Princess Diana. Now a new Panorama team fronted by journalist John Ware has carried out its own four-month investigation, with exclusive interviews and revelations from internal BBC documents. This is the inside story of how Martin Bashir obtained his career-defining interview and how the BBC responded when it discovered he'd faked bank statements and shown them to Diana's brother, Earl Spencer

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on what happens when care goes wrong in hospitals, revealing how some NHS trusts seemingly keep critical reports hidden from the regulator and the public. Repeated scandals have shown how transparency is vital to make sure lessons are learned. However, after a year-long investigation uncovering around 100 unpublished independent reports, reporter and GP Faye Kirkland discovers how hospital failings are apparently being buried

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary following how British Muslims mark some of life's biggest moments. Married couple She and Ali look back at their vows and reflect on how their faith has helped them in their marriage. Emine marks the death of her mother with a final prayer, symbolising her mum coming from god, and going back to god

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Roman Catholics Maria and Peter have their fifth child baptised in a traditional Latin service and in Stockton-on-Tees family and friends pay their last respects to June, one of the first women to be ordained as a priest by the Church of the England in 1994

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwall's most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britain's greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: John Williams at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances featuring the guitarist, from a BBC archive spanning 15 years. The programme includes classical masterworks, the prog rock of Sky and comedy with Eric Sykes. There are also duets with fellow guitar maestro Julian Bream

programme.title: Sound Waves: The Symphony of Physics
programme.desc: Part two of two. Helen Czerski explores the extraordinary messages carried by sound waves, and how they can help humans gain a better understanding of the world around them

programme.title: Dark Matter: A History of the Afrofuture
programme.desc: Science-fiction and fantastical influences on the work of black artists around the world, examining how they address the horrors of the past while imagining alternative futures. The film explores the work of techno musicians Drexciya, visual artists Ellen Gallagher and Hew Locke, visionary musician Sun Ra, multidisciplinary artist Tavares Strachan, writer Ekow Eshun and painter Kerry James Marshall

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Afua Hirsch explores three African countries through their art, music and culture, beginning by charting Ethiopia's 3,000-year history

programme.title: The Great Butterfly Adventure: Africa to Britain with the Painted Lady
programme.desc: The migration of the painted lady butterfly has long fascinated scientists, artists and nature lovers alike. The longest butterfly migration on Earth, it sees millions of these delicate creatures travel from the desert fringes of North Africa, across thousands of miles of land and sea, before settling in the UK. However, the migration has never truly been understood - until now. Here, Martha Kearney and entomologist Dr James Logan reveal the secrets of this extraordinary phenomenon, employing ground-breaking techniques as they unlock the secrets of the painted ladies

programme.title: Sound Waves: The Symphony of Physics
programme.desc: Part two of two. Helen Czerski explores the extraordinary messages carried by sound waves, and how they can help humans gain a better understanding of the world around them

programme.title: Dark Matter: A History of the Afrofuture
programme.desc: Science-fiction and fantastical influences on the work of black artists around the world, examining how they address the horrors of the past while imagining alternative futures. The film explores the work of techno musicians Drexciya, visual artists Ellen Gallagher and Hew Locke, visionary musician Sun Ra, multidisciplinary artist Tavares Strachan, writer Ekow Eshun and painter Kerry James Marshall

programme.title: John Williams at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances featuring the guitarist, from a BBC archive spanning 15 years. The programme includes classical masterworks, the prog rock of Sky and comedy with Eric Sykes. There are also duets with fellow guitar maestro Julian Bream

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but experiences engine problems on the way

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross uses striking blue tones in this oval-shaped painting of a secluded cabin on a winter's night

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: Film-maker Rankin sets a series of challenges for six talented amateur photographers. In the first round, they are tested on the speed of their reactions on Brighton seafront, where they must capture stunning images using just a smart phone. They are then joined by Chris Packham in the New Forest to take shots of wildlife, and finally take a celebrity portrait of Anna Friel

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: The arts critic and journalist interviews the renowned photographer, who is responsible for capturing some of the images that have defined the past 60 years

programme.title: Britain in Focus: A Photographic History
programme.desc: Eamonn McCabe explores the development of the art of photography in the UK, beginning by looking at how science and technology allowed pioneering photographers like Roger Fenton and Julia Margaret Cameron to create a new art form in the 1800s

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Profile of photographer Dennis Morris, to coincide with an exhibition of his work with Public Image Limited opening at the Institute of Contemporary Arts. The film follows Morris as he works on a photoshoot of punk band Skinny Girl Diet. He reflects on the highlights of his past career, which included documenting the punk scene and London's black community in the 1960s and 1970s, as well as capturing images of Bob Marley and the Sex Pistols on tour

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross uses striking blue tones in this oval-shaped painting of a secluded cabin on a winter's night

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but experiences engine problems on the way

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: Film-maker Rankin sets a series of challenges for six talented amateur photographers. In the first round, they are tested on the speed of their reactions on Brighton seafront, where they must capture stunning images using just a smart phone. They are then joined by Chris Packham in the New Forest to take shots of wildlife, and finally take a celebrity portrait of Anna Friel

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the people who operated the canal boats, carrying fuel and goods around the country. During the Victorian era they gained reputations for criminality, violence and drinking, but was such a reputation deserved? Liz discovers grisly canal crimes, investigates health and welfare on-board working boats, and looks at why canal children were last on the list to be offered safeguards and formal education. She reveals the campaigners who set out to tackle this injustice, including George Smith of Coalville, Leicestershire, and Sister Mary Ward of Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an elegant mountain lake, silent and still, reflecting the natural beauty of leafy trees and grasses all around it

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Jim Hacker is disturbed to discover his department is responsible for supplying surveillance equipment to the government, which is then used to spy on its own ministers. However, a death threat soon changes his view on the situation. Political satire, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey reprimands DeVere for not fulfilling his duties as lord of the manor when he fails to attend church on Sunday, but then forgets to practise what she preaches. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The satirist explores the nation's obsession with the past to reveal how and why the British have continually plundered the 'olden days' to make sense of and shape the present. He begins by examining the various spins put on the lives of King Arthur and Alfred the Great through the ages. He finds out how Arthur has been variously depicted as a wild Celtic warlord, a chivalric hero and a piously questing king, while Alfred's character was manipulated to suit the purposes of tricky medieval lawyers, a Tudor archbishop and a Georgian Prince of Wales

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: Steven Johnson and David Olusoga explore the remarkable and often unsung work that has led to major medical advances. Alexander Fleming's famous discovery of antibiotics was followed - in the midst of the Second World War - by the rollout of the manufacture of penicillin at scale, so that lives might be saved at a global level. Pioneering rational drug design in the 1980s led to the development of antiviral drugs for HIV and other previously untreatable diseases. The knowledge of how to produce safe, effective drugs now underpins the work to find treatments for new diseases, such as Covid-19

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: As they contain no active ingredient, placebo medicines and pills should not really work, but they are now being shown to be effective in helping treat pain and depression and even alleviating some of the symptoms of Parkinson's disease. This programme explores why they work and how everyone could benefit from them

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Michael Mosley investigates mankind's earliest attempts to tackle infection and reveals how microbes are used to fight against disease. The programme also examines how scientists, chemists and doctors, from Louis Pasteur to Howard Florey, each contributed to these medical breakthroughs, and there is a look at the eradication of smallpox

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford examines the story of Andreas Vesalius, who in 1537 became the most famous anatomist in Europe. He went on to produce the first complete account of the human body and how to dissect it, and his drawings set the standard for anatomical art for centuries to come, earning him the title of 'the founder of modern anatomy'

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an elegant mountain lake, silent and still, reflecting the natural beauty of leafy trees and grasses all around it

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the people who operated the canal boats, carrying fuel and goods around the country. programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse's over-reliance on gadgets prompts Colonel K to send his top agent on a tech-free nature retreat. Unbeknownst to Danger Mouse, Greenback has the same plans for his daughter, Delilah, whose social media obsession has also spiralled out of control. Back at HQ Penfold is tasked with protecting the entire world from the threat of super-villainy. Stripped off his gadgets, DM struggles to adapt to life in the wilderness and, when a talking pinecone called Conni begins to offer him survival tips, he begins to question his own sanity. Soon enough, Delilah catches wind of DM's presence and she plots to destroy him as a means of getting back into her Dad's good books. With the voices of Alexander Armstrong and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Historical characters and events are explored in sketches and quizzes based on the books by Terry Deary. Comedy talent including Sarah Hadland, Steve Punt and Meera Syal help bring the strange facts, gory battles and crazy inventions to life

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A special episode about the life of Mary, Queen of Scots, following her as she stumbles from young girl in Scotland to queen in France, back to queen in Scotland, and finally to Elizabeth I's arch enemy. Plus, the programme meets silver-nosed Danish scientist Tycho Brahe, and Mr H gives his unique take on the epic Battle of Lepanto in another edition of History's Craziest Fools

programme.title: Gangsta Granny
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Schoolboy Ben is bored witless after being made to stay with his grandma. All she ever wants to do is play Scrabble and eat yucky cabbage soup. But it turns out there's a whole lot more to this OAP than cardigans and toffees when she tells him how she used to be an international jewel thief - and when she admits there was one heist she never quite managed in her heyday, the pair set out on a criminal adventure. Julia McKenzie stars as the shady senior citizen, with Walliams and Miranda Hart as the boys' ballroom-dancing parents, alongside Joanna Lumley, Rob Brydon and Robbie Williams, with Reece Buttery as Ben

programme.title: Got What It Takes?
programme.desc: Anna Maynard welcomes new co-host Melvin Odoom and this year’s talented teen singers and their mums to the Music Mansion.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The boys welcome some new arrivals to the zoo. Mimo the Pygmy Marmoset emerges from its nest box for the first time and two penguin chicks hatch. Meanwhile, it’s Tilly the red panda’s birthday, and to celebrate, the brothers decide to hold a cake-off. It’s a tradition at the zoo that when a new animal in born, all the animals get presents. Finally, we catch up with the boys in Rwanda as they meet wildlife heroes fighting to save the planet’s largest primates - the mountain gorilla.

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to unearth the dinosaurs that were far deadlier than they first appeared. He discovers how the Sinornithosaurus hides a deadly dental secret, races a Velociraptor in a climbing challenge and puts Argentinasaurus to the ultimate test

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi are young black teenagers who have all experienced racism growing up in the UK. Mandi has been called racist names, Isaiah is often followed round shops by security guards, and Zionne feels she will always have to work harder than everyone else to succeed. In the first of a two-part special, they speak out about what life is really like growing up black in the UK and why things urgently need to change. After the death of George Floyd and the nationwide protests against racism, the teenagers know that this is their time to be heard. As well as opening up about their own experiences, they speak to their friends and their parents. They decide to create a manifesto to list the big things they feel need to change in society to make life better for black kids like them.

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on a journey of discovery to find out about plants around the world. Find out how we can help to look after them and our planet, and why and how they grow and survive in different climates. Maddie explores plants in the rainforest, big and small, and sees how the clever drip-tip leaves work watering the plants beneath them. We’ll see how plants in hot, dry parts of world like the desert thrive in their own special ways, like the cactus plant that stores litres of water in its waxy leaves. And we’ll learn how plants that grow close to home in the UK, can grow in a colder and wetter climate.

When Matthew arrives at a park, he gets the chance to ride a steam train and find out how they make it go! We then follow Emily as she attends a Baby yoga class with her mum and sister. These include a bunny-shaped rock, a cloud-shaped leaf and a heart-shaped stone. But he doesn't notice that each time he take a souvenir, it disturbs a forest creature that was using it. Once he finally realises this, he and Book Cub work together to put all of the treasures back in their rightful places.

Back at home, Kyra and Khloe become chefs and help Mum make a special dinner for the whole family.

Next, Maddie joins a road-building team to see how a motorway is made of different layers and uses a special thermal imaging camera to see how the hot asphalt is laid

Dr Piggles asks Love Monster if he can fill in for him while he rests, assuring him that he'll be right there to help out. But when the hiccups get worse, he has to lock himself in the back room, and Love Monster must find ways to make all the patients laugh by himself.
